links.targetTitle: Swarm manifests form a three-tier hierarchy of user, drive, and document feeds keyed by deterministic keccak256 topics.

global.title: Document manifest feed holds latestRevision per scope and ordered operationBatches array
global.content:
# Document Manifest Feed Structure

Each document has its own manifest feed at topic `ph:v2:doc:<docId>`, owned by the document creator's signer. Each feed index points to encrypted JSON on `/bytes`.

## Schema

```json
{
  "documentId": "doc-001",
  "documentType": "powerhouse/budget-statement",
  "latestRevision": { "global": 12 },
  "operationBatches": [
    {
      "reference": "ddd444...",
      "scope": "global",
      "branch": "main",
      "startIndex": 0,
      "endIndex": 5,
      "timestamp": "2026-04-10T12:01:00Z"
    }
  ],
  "keyframes": [],
  "updatedAt": "2026-04-10T12:03:00Z"
}
```

## Role

- Points to a list of operation batches, each an immutable `/bytes` reference
- `latestRevision` tracks the highest operation index per scope
- Batches are appended, not rewritten — new operations get a new batch entry
- Clients reconstruct full state by fetching each batch in order and replaying
global.description: Document manifest feed at ph:v2:doc:<docId> holds latestRevision per scope, operationBatches array (reference, scope, branch, startIndex, endIndex), and keyframes.
